title: Shri Badrinath Temple, Uttarakhand
intro: The Shri Badrinath Temple, located in the Chamoli district of Uttarakhand, is one of the holiest and most famous pilgrimage sites in India dedicated to Lord Vishnu.
description:
Shri Badrinath Temple – Uttarakhand
Introduction
The Shri Badrinath Temple, located in the Chamoli district of Uttarakhand, is one of the holiest and most famous pilgrimage sites in India dedicated to Lord Vishnu. It is a major part of the Char Dham Yatra and the Chota Char Dham Yatra.
Lord Vishnu resides here in the form of Badri Narayan. Nestled amidst beautiful Himalayan mountain ranges, this temple is a center of faith, devotion, and spiritual peace for millions of devotees.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Badrinath Temple
 Other Names: Badrinath Dham, Badri Vishal Temple
 Location: Badrinath, Chamoli District, Uttarakhand 246422, India
 Presiding Deity: Lord Badri Narayan (Lord Vishnu)
 Significance: One of the Char Dhams
 Nearest Major City: Dehradun (approx. 320 km)
History and Significance
Badrinath Dham is one of the most prominent pilgrimage sites for Lord Vishnu. According to religious beliefs, Lord Vishnu performed penance at this location, which is why it is considered extremely sacred.
Situated on the banks of the Alaknanda River in the Himalayan region, this temple holds special significance in the Vaishnava tradition of Hinduism. The pilgrimage to Badrinath Dham is considered a means to attain spiritual growth and the grace of Lord Vishnu.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Jolly Grant Airport, Dehradun: Approx. 310 km from Badrinath. Taxis and buses are available from the airport to reach Badrinath.
🚆 By Train
 Rishikesh Railway Station: Approx. 295 km from Badrinath. Buses and taxis are available from Rishikesh to reach Badrinath by road.
🛣️ By Road
Badrinath is connected by road to Rishikesh, Haridwar, Dehradun, and Joshimath. Travelers can reach here by bus, taxi, or private vehicle.
Best Time to Visit
The Badrinath Temple remains closed in winter due to heavy snowfall.
Good time for the Yatra:
 May to June
 September to October
The weather is more favorable for travel during these months.
Main Festivals
 Mata Murti Ka Mela: A major religious festival of Badrinath Dham.
 Badri-Kedar Festival: A special festival associated with the Badrinath and Kedarnath Dhams.
 Janmashtami: The festival celebrating the birth of Lord Shri Krishna.
Temple Timings
🕒 4:30 AM to 9:00 PM
(General timings during the pilgrimage season; timings may change on festivals and special occasions.)
Nearby Attractions
 Tapt Kund: A sacred hot water spring near the temple where devotees take a holy dip.
 Mana Village: Known as the last Indian village near the India-Tibet border.
 Vyas Gufa: It is believed that Maharishi Ved Vyas composed the Mahabharata here.
 Bhim Pul: A natural stone bridge located over the Saraswati River.
 Charan Paduka: A sacred site believed to hold the footprints of Lord Vishnu.
Dress Code
Considering the cold weather in Badrinath:
 Wear warm and comfortable clothes.
 Carry jackets, woolens, and rain protection gear.
 Modest and traditional attire is considered appropriate.
Facilities for Devotees
 Darshan arrangements
 Accommodation facilities
 Food and Prasad arrangements
 Medical assistance
 Help centers along the pilgrimage route
 Local transport facilities
Conclusion
The Shri Badrinath Temple is one of the holiest abodes of Lord Vishnu. Nestled in the serene Himalayas, this temple is a wonderful center of faith, devotion, and spiritual experience. For devotees, the Badrinath Yatra is not just a pilgrimage, but a sacred spiritual journey of faith and dedication toward Lord Vishnu.

faq.q1: To which deity is the Badrinath Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: The Badrinath Temple is dedicated to Lord Vishnu in the form of Badri Narayan.
faq.q2: Where is the Badrinath Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in the Chamoli district, Uttarakhand.
faq.q3: Of which pilgrimage is Badrinath Dham a part?
faq.a3: It is a major part of India's Char Dham Yatra.
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit Badrinath?
faq.a4: May to June and September to October are considered good months for the pilgrimage.
faq.q5: Which places can one visit near Badrinath?
faq.a5: Tapt Kund, Mana Village, Vyas Gufa, Bhim Pul, and Charan Paduka are the major places to visit.
